hero.kicker: — on my way to become obsolete
hero.lede: devops manager at palo alto networks. i build agents that do my job, so i can go build the next one. the goal is to make myself unnecessary. that is the whole job.
hero.lede_cv: boss at palo alto networks. build robot. robot do job. build next robot.

days.detail: 10 commits · the work repo + undisclosed projects
12 commits · the work repo (the reviews never stop)
11 commits · the work repo + side quests

projects.name: $DAYJOB
projects.desc: add a label to a PR. 30 seconds later, it posts a structured review. running quietly inside a private github enterprise — 194 reviews last week across 56 repos. strips sycophancy on parse, so it never tells you your code is “great”.
projects.desc_cv: put label on PR. agent appear. agent review. agent not compliment. agent honest.
projects.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 380" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" style="width:100%;height:100%;padding:32px 40px;box-sizing:border-box;"><g font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="12"><text x="0" y="22" fill="var(--text-muted)" font-size="10">// diff · reviewer/core.py</text><text x="0" y="52" fill="var(--text-soft)">+ def review_pr(diff):</text><text x="0" y="74" fill="var(--text-soft)">+   findings = []</text><text x="0" y="96" fill="var(--text-soft)">+   for hunk in diff.hunks:</text><text x="0" y="118" fill="var(--accent)" font-weight="700">-     issues = check(hunk)</text><text x="0" y="140" fill="var(--status-good)">+     issues = audit(hunk)</text><text x="0" y="162" fill="var(--text-soft)">+     findings += issues</text><text x="0" y="184" fill="var(--text-soft)">+   return findings</text></g><path d="M -10 118 L -14 118 L -14 248 L 30 248" stroke="var(--accent)" stroke-width="2" fill="none" fill="var(--accent)"/></marker></defs><rect x="40" y="230" width="340" height="52" rx="8" fill="var(--bg-paper)" stroke="var(--accent)" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="56" y="251" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="11" fill="var(--text-primary)" font-weight="700">$dayjob · L4 severity</text><text x="56" y="269" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="10" projects.name: brif
projects.desc: a mission-aware statusline for claude code. tells you when context is dying. shows goal, cost, model. asks before it does anything irreversible — that APPROVE button is the only feature i actually care about.
projects.desc_cv: bar at bottom. bar say 'context die soon'. bar ask before explode. me like bar.
projects.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 280 110" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;height:100%;"><rect x="0" y="0" width="280" height="110" fill="var(--invert-bg)"/><text x="14" y="34" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="11" fill="var(--invert-text)">opus-4.8 · main · $dayjob</text><text x="14" y="55" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="11" fill="var(--accent)">ctx 87% ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░  $0.42  14m</text><text x="14" y="75" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="10" fill="var(--accent-2)">└─ APPROVE? edit reviewer/app.py:124</text><text x="14" y="95" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="10" fill="var(--text-muted)">  [y]es  [n]o  [d]iff</text></svg>

projects.name: skit
projects.desc: the npm of AICs. install once, works in claude code AND cursor AND windsurf. started on a tuesday afternoon when i should have been doing something else.
projects.desc_cv: npm but robot. one install. all editor. built on tuesday. should not have.
projects.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;height:100%;padding:14px 18px;box-sizing:border-box;"><rect x="0" y="4" width="36" height="36" rx="6" fill="var(--accent)"/><text x="18" y="28" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="15" font-weight="700" fill="#fff">$</text><text x="46" y="20" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="12" fill="var(--text-primary)" font-weight="500">skit install jira-mcp</text><text x="46" y="40" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="11" fill="var(--status-good)">✓ verified · 1.2.3 → 1.4.0</text></svg>

projects.name: amit-hub
projects.desc: git worktrees an agent can drive without losing its mind. CLI + VS Code ext + claude code MCP plugin — one TS core, three surfaces. a colleague built the same thing privately before i shipped. that's how i knew it was real.
projects.desc_cv: agent get own branch. agent not confused. colleague build same. that mean good idea.
projects.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 110" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;height:100%;padding:14px 18px;box-sizing:border-box;"><rect x="0" y="10" width="52" height="56" rx="4" fill="var(--bg-paper)" stroke="var(--line-base)" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="64" y="10" width="52" height="56" rx="4" fill="var(--bg-paper)" stroke="var(--line-base)" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="128" y="10" width="52" height="56" rx="4" fill="var(--accent)" stroke="var(--accent-deep)" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="26" y="43" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="9" fill="var(--text-primary)" font-weight="600">main</text><text x="90" y="43" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="9" fill="var(--text-primary)" font-weight="600">feat-1</text><text x="154" y="43" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="9" fill="#fff" font-weight="600">agent</text><text x="0" y="84" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="9" fill="var(--text-muted)">each branch: isolated context</text></svg>

projects.name: workwright
projects.desc: playwright, but for terminals. an MCP server that lets an agent drive a real PTY — run a command, watch the output stream, react. the thing claude code couldn't do until it could.
projects.desc_cv: playwright but terminal. agent type command. agent watch output. agent react. very useful.
projects.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 280 110" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;height:100%;"><rect x="0" y="0" width="280" height="110" fill="var(--invert-bg)"/><text x="14" y="34" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="11" fill="var(--status-good)">$ workwright run "npm test"</text><text x="14" y="56" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="10" fill="var(--invert-text)">▸ pty spawned · agent attached</text><text x="14" y="76" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="10" fill="var(--accent-2)">▸ 14 passed · 0 failed</text><text x="14" y="96" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="10" fill="var(--text-muted)">└─ streamed back to the model</text></svg>

projects.name: $STANDUP
projects.desc: every meeting ends with a transcript, a summary, and a list of action items. runs locally. never touches a cloud you don't control.
projects.desc_cv: meeting happen. robot listen. robot write notes. notes accurate. meeting over.
projects.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 280 110" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;height:100%;"><rect x="0" y="0" width="280" height="110" fill="var(--invert-bg)"/><text x="14" y="28" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="11" fill="var(--invert-text)">▸ transcribing standup.m4a</text><rect x="14" y="38" width="200" height="6" rx="3" fill="var(--line-base)" opacity="0.4"/><rect x="14" y="38" width="163" height="6" rx="3" fill="var(--accent)"/><text x="220" y="46" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="9" fill="var(--accent)">81%</text><text x="14" y="68" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="10" fill="var(--status-good)">✓ 1,240 words · 14 action items</text><text x="14" y="90" font-family="Space Mono, monospace" font-size="10" fill="var(--text-muted)">└─ written to standup-2026-w26.md</text></svg>

projects.name: whetstone
projects.desc: the system that keeps me on scope. cron-driven agent jobs, drift detection, session discipline. a mentor that runs on pm2 and never lets me spiral.
projects.desc_cv: robot watch me work. robot say 'stay on task'. robot right. me annoyed. me productive.

writing.title: short memory.
writing.excerpt: “Too dangerous to release” is a press release, not a safety decision. The fact stays. The feeling wipes clean.

writing.title: the context bar.
writing.excerpt: Context window saturation is silent degradation. I built a monitoring system for a machine that does my work.

writing.title: the package manager nobody asked for.
writing.excerpt: AI assistants quietly became a runtime. Runtimes always get a package manager. Someone always gets annoyed enough.

writing.title: things i didn't know.
writing.excerpt: Working effectively with AI coding agents is not a coding skill. It is an architectural, process, and delegation skill.
